meta.disclaimer: Cited evidence synthesis for clinician decision support and not medical advice. Prognosis figures are population ranges, not individual predictions.

defining_molecular_features: IDH-wildtype status (no IDH1/IDH2 mutation) is required

Grade 4 assigned by microvascular proliferation and/or necrosis, OR by molecular criteria even without them: TERT promoter mutation, EGFR amplification, or combined gain of whole chromosome 7 and loss of whole chromosome 10 (+7/-10)
entities.classification_basis: WHO CNS5 restricts 'glioblastoma' to IDH-wildtype astrocytic tumors. An IDH-wildtype, histologically lower-grade diffuse astrocytoma is diagnosed as grade 4 glioblastoma if it carries any of TERT promoter mutation, EGFR amplification, or +7/-10 (cIMPACT-NOW update 3).
entities.clinical_significance: Most common and most aggressive adult diffuse glioma; the molecular grade-4 criteria capture aggressive tumors that would otherwise be undergraded on histology alone.
entities.uncertainty_notes: The three molecular grade-4 markers are treated as equivalent in guidelines, but their independent prognostic weight in tumors lacking histologic anaplasia continues to be refined.

citations.title: The 2021 WHO Classification of Tumors of the Central Nervous System: a summary
cIMPACT-NOW update 3: recommended diagnostic criteria for 'Diffuse astrocytic glioma, IDH-wildtype, with molecular features of glioblastoma, WHO grade IV'
Glioma Groups Based on 1p/19q, IDH, and TERT Promoter Mutations in Tumors

defining_molecular_features: ATRX loss and/or TP53 mutation typical; absence of 1p/19q codeletion (distinguishes from oligodendroglioma)
CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion defines WHO grade 4 within this entity regardless of histology
entities.classification_basis: WHO CNS5 groups IDH-mutant astrocytomas as a single entity graded 2-4. Grading is integrated: CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion mandates grade 4 even without necrosis or microvascular proliferation.
entities.clinical_significance: Better prognosis than IDH-wildtype glioblastoma at matched grade; grade and CDKN2A/B status stratify outcome and increasingly guide management.
entities.uncertainty_notes: Optimal grade cutoffs and the full set of grade-4 molecular markers within IDH-mutant astrocytoma remain under active refinement.

IDH1 and IDH2 mutations in gliomas
Novel, improved grading system(s) for IDH-mutant astrocytic gliomas
Translational significance of CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion in IDH-mutant astrocytoma

defining_molecular_features: IDH1 or IDH2 mutation AND whole-arm codeletion of 1p and 19q (both required)
Frequent TERT promoter mutation; ATRX and TP53 typically retained/wildtype
entities.classification_basis: WHO CNS5 requires BOTH IDH mutation and 1p/19q codeletion for the diagnosis; graded 2 or 3. Codeletion is a defining, not merely prognostic, feature.
entities.clinical_significance: Best prognosis of the three adult-type diffuse gliomas and the most chemosensitive; 1p/19q codeletion predicts benefit from PCV chemotherapy added to radiotherapy.
entities.uncertainty_notes: Molecular grade-4 criteria applied to astrocytomas are not established for codeleted oligodendroglioma; a formal WHO grade 4 is not currently defined for this entity.

biomarkers.name: IDH1 / IDH2 mutation
biomarkers.what_it_is: Point mutations (most commonly IDH1 R132H) in isocitrate dehydrogenase producing the oncometabolite D-2-hydroxyglutarate, which reshapes the tumor epigenome.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: The top-level branch point of adult diffuse glioma classification; IDH-mutant tumors have markedly better outcomes than IDH-wildtype at matched grade.
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: Appears as inclusion/exclusion by entity (e.g., 'IDH-wildtype glioblastoma' required, or 'IDH-mutant' required for IDH-inhibitor trials) and as a stratification factor.
assay_methods: DNA sequencing (Sanger / NGS) for non-canonical IDH1/IDH2 variants
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: IHC detects only canonical R132H; sequencing is required to exclude IDH mutation in younger patients or IHC-negative cases.

citations.title: IDH1 and IDH2 mutations in gliomas
The 2021 WHO Classification of Tumors of the Central Nervous System: a summary

biomarkers.name: MGMT promoter methylation
biomarkers.what_it_is: Epigenetic silencing of the MGMT DNA-repair gene, reducing removal of temozolomide-induced O6-methylguanine lesions.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: Predictive of temozolomide benefit and prognostic in glioblastoma; methylated tumors respond better to alkylating chemotherapy.
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: Commonly a stratification factor in newly diagnosed GBM trials; sometimes an inclusion criterion (methylated-only or unmethylated-only cohorts).
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: No universal assay cutoff; borderline/low-level methylation is interpreted inconsistently across labs and platforms.

citations.title: MGMT gene silencing and benefit from temozolomide in glioblastoma
Radiotherapy plus concomitant and adjuvant temozolomide for glioblastoma
Clinical Neuropathology practice guide 5-2015: MGMT methylation pyrosequencing in glioblastoma

biomarkers.name: EGFR (amplification / EGFRvIII)
biomarkers.what_it_is: EGFR gene amplification and the constitutively active EGFRvIII deletion variant, common oncogenic drivers in IDH-wildtype glioblastoma.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: EGFR amplification is one of the molecular criteria defining grade-4 IDH-wildtype glioblastoma; EGFRvIII is a tumor-specific target for CAR-T and other immunotherapies.
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: An inclusion/enrichment criterion in EGFR-targeted trials ('EGFR-amplified' or 'EGFRvIII-positive'); also contributes to molecular grade-4 assignment.
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: EGFRvIII expression is spatially heterogeneous and can be lost under targeted pressure; independent prognostic value of EGFR status is debated.

citations.title: Epidermal growth factor receptor and EGFRvIII in glioblastoma: signaling pathways and targeted therapies
Prognostic value of epidermal growth factor receptor amplification and EGFRvIII in glioblastoma
cIMPACT-NOW update 3: recommended diagnostic criteria for 'Diffuse astrocytic glioma, IDH-wildtype, with molecular features of glioblastoma, WHO grade IV'

biomarkers.name: 1p/19q codeletion
biomarkers.what_it_is: Combined whole-arm loss of chromosomes 1p and 19q, arising from an unbalanced translocation.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: Defining feature of oligodendroglioma (with IDH mutation) and predictive of benefit from PCV chemotherapy added to radiotherapy.
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: Used to confirm/require the oligodendroglioma diagnosis or to exclude it; appears as 'IDH-mutant, 1p/19q-codeleted' or its negation.
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: Partial (non-whole-arm) 1p/19q loss does not qualify and can mislead FISH; whole-arm confirmation is preferred.

biomarkers.name: ATRX
biomarkers.what_it_is: Chromatin-remodeling gene; loss-of-function mutation leads to loss of nuclear ATRX protein and the alternative-lengthening-of-telomeres phenotype.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: ATRX loss supports an astrocytic, IDH-mutant lineage and helps distinguish astrocytoma from oligodendroglioma (which retains ATRX).
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: Rarely an explicit eligibility gate; used diagnostically to define the entity a trial enrolls.
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: ATRX IHC can be technically difficult to interpret; mosaic or retained staining does not fully exclude mutation.

citations.title: IDH mutation, 1p19q codeletion and ATRX loss in WHO grade II gliomas
The 2021 WHO Classification of Tumors of the Central Nervous System: a summary

biomarkers.name: TP53 mutation
biomarkers.what_it_is: Mutation of the TP53 tumor-suppressor gene, frequent in IDH-mutant astrocytoma.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: Together with ATRX loss, supports the IDH-mutant astrocytoma lineage; largely mutually exclusive with 1p/19q codeletion.
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: Used diagnostically rather than as a stand-alone eligibility criterion.
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: IHC over-/under-expression is only a surrogate; sequencing confirms mutation status.

citations.title: Molecular Profiling Reveals Biologically Discrete Subsets and Pathways of Progression in Diffuse Glioma
The 2021 WHO Classification of Tumors of the Central Nervous System: a summary

biomarkers.name: TERT promoter mutation
biomarkers.what_it_is: Hotspot mutations (C228T/C250T) in the TERT promoter that reactivate telomerase.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: In IDH-wildtype astrocytic tumors, one of the molecular criteria defining grade-4 glioblastoma; also frequent in codeleted oligodendroglioma. Prognostic effect is context-dependent.
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: Contributes to molecular grade-4 GBM diagnosis; less often an explicit trial gate.
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: Prognostic direction differs by IDH and 1p/19q context - adverse in IDH-wildtype astrocytoma but not uniformly so elsewhere; evolving evidence.

Human TERT promoter mutations as a prognostic biomarker in glioma

biomarkers.name: CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion
biomarkers.what_it_is: Biallelic (homozygous) deletion of the CDKN2A/CDKN2B tumor-suppressor locus on 9p21.
biomarkers.clinical_significance: In IDH-mutant astrocytoma, homozygous deletion mandates WHO grade 4 and marks a substantially worse prognosis.
biomarkers.eligibility_notes: Increasingly captured for grading/stratification of IDH-mutant astrocytoma cohorts.
biomarkers.uncertainty_notes: Heterozygous loss does not carry the same grade-4 implication; assay sensitivity for true homozygous deletion varies.

drug_class_exclusions.why_excluded: Prior anti-VEGF therapy confounds MRI response assessment (pseudoresponse from vascular normalization), can alter recurrence biology, and overlaps with bleeding/wound-healing and hypertension toxicities; many trials therefore exclude prior bevacizumab or use it as a stratifier.
typical_criterion_wording: Prior treatment with bevacizumab or other anti-VEGF/anti-angiogenic agents
No prior VEGF/VEGFR-targeted therapy for the current tumor
drug_class_exclusions.uncertainty_notes: Bevacizumab did not extend overall survival in newly diagnosed GBM (AVAglio, RTOG 0825) though it improved progression-free survival; whether prior exposure should exclude vs stratify varies by trial design.

citations.title: Bevacizumab plus radiotherapy-temozolomide for newly diagnosed glioblastoma
A randomized trial of bevacizumab for newly diagnosed glioblastoma

population_prognosis.measure: median overall survival under standard care (maximal safe resection + radiotherapy with concurrent/adjuvant temozolomide)
population_prognosis.population_range: ~15-16 months median OS in the pivotal trial population; roughly 12-15 months in unselected real-world cohorts
modifiers: MGMT-methylated tumors survive substantially longer than unmethylated
adding tumor-treating fields to maintenance TMZ improved median OS to ~20-21 months in trial
younger age and higher performance status favorable
population_prognosis.uncertainty_notes: Trial populations are selected and outperform real-world cohorts; figures are population ranges under standard care and must not be read as an individual patient's expected survival.

population_prognosis.measure: median overall survival by grade under standard care
population_prognosis.population_range: Grade 2-3 commonly measured in years to over a decade; grade 4 (incl. CDKN2A/B-deleted) markedly shorter, though still longer than IDH-wildtype glioblastoma at matched grade
modifiers: WHO grade is the dominant modifier
CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion shortens survival and defines grade 4
extent of resection and adjuvant chemoradiotherapy
population_prognosis.uncertainty_notes: Wide survival spread across grades 2-4; ranges are population-level and depend heavily on grade and molecular features - not an individual prediction.

population_prognosis.measure: median overall survival under radiotherapy plus PCV chemotherapy
population_prognosis.population_range: Longest among adult diffuse gliomas - median OS on the order of ~14 years for anaplastic (grade 3) codeleted tumors treated with RT plus PCV in randomized trials; grade 2 generally longer
modifiers: 1p/19q codeletion predicts chemosensitivity and long survival
adding PCV to radiotherapy improved overall survival
population_prognosis.uncertainty_notes: Long-term trial estimates (RTOG 9402, EORTC 26951) come from anaplastic cohorts and predate full molecular selection; population ranges, not individual predictions.

trial_rationale.target_or_mechanism: Selinexor - selective inhibitor of nuclear export (SINE) blocking XPO1/CRM1 - combined with the alkylator temozolomide in recurrent glioblastoma
trial_rationale.scientific_rationale: XPO1/CRM1 inhibition traps tumor-suppressor proteins in the nucleus and disrupts oncoprotein export; single-agent selinexor showed CNS penetration and durable responses in a subset of recurrent GBM, supporting combination with temozolomide to enhance alkylator-induced cytotoxicity.
landmark_evidence: Phase II single-agent oral selinexor in recurrent GBM demonstrated intratumoral drug penetration and a subset with durable radiographic response (Lassman et al., Clin Cancer Res 2021, PMID 34728525)
Temozolomide is the established alkylating backbone from the Stupp regimen (PMID 15758009)

citations.title: A Phase II Study of the Efficacy and Safety of Oral Selinexor in Recurrent Glioblastoma
Radiotherapy plus concomitant and adjuvant temozolomide for glioblastoma

trial_rationale.target_or_mechanism: ERAS-801 - a brain-penetrant small-molecule EGFR inhibitor - in surgically accessible, EGFR-amplified/mutated, IDH-wildtype recurrent glioblastoma
trial_rationale.scientific_rationale: EGFR amplification/mutation is a dominant oncogenic driver and a molecular grade-4 criterion in IDH-wildtype glioblastoma; prior EGFR inhibitors failed partly due to poor CNS exposure, so a CNS-penetrant EGFR inhibitor is tested with a surgical (window-of-opportunity) design in EGFR-altered tumors to confirm target engagement.
landmark_evidence: EGFR amplification/EGFRvIII biology as a GBM driver and target (PMID 29321659)
EGFR amplification is one of the molecular grade-4 defining alterations (cIMPACT-NOW update 3, PMID 30259105)

citations.title: Epidermal growth factor receptor and EGFRvIII in glioblastoma: signaling pathways and targeted therapies
cIMPACT-NOW update 3: recommended diagnostic criteria for 'Diffuse astrocytic glioma, IDH-wildtype, with molecular features of glioblastoma, WHO grade IV'

trial_rationale.target_or_mechanism: CART-EGFR-IL13Ralpha2 - bivalent chimeric antigen receptor T cells targeting EGFR and IL13Ralpha2 - in EGFR-amplified recurrent glioblastoma
trial_rationale.scientific_rationale: Single-target CAR-T against EGFRvIII produced antigen loss and adaptive resistance; dual targeting of EGFR and IL13Ralpha2 addresses antigen heterogeneity and escape, and intrathecal bivalent CAR-T has produced early radiographic responses in recurrent GBM, motivating optimization of dosing/delivery.
landmark_evidence: Single-dose EGFRvIII CAR-T showed antigen loss and adaptive resistance in recurrent GBM (O'Rourke et al., Sci Transl Med 2017, PMID 28724573)
IL13Ralpha2-targeted CAR-T produced a documented tumor regression (Brown et al., NEJM 2016, PMID 28029927)
Intrathecal bivalent EGFR/IL13Ralpha2 CAR-T yielded early responses in recurrent GBM (Bagley et al., Nature Medicine 2024, PMID 38480922)
